ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for tissue prata or roti prata. FOODON_00002682 (flatbread food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW (Flat bread) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for roti prata or tissue prata in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for tissue prata or roti prata specifically. No suitable parent concept identified for clinical coding purposes.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving of tissue prata with honey is approximately 120 g, comprising one large paper-thin crispy cone-shaped prata drizzled with honey, as typically served at Singapore hawker stalls and Indian-Muslim restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Roti Prata (Plain) — HPB Singapore Foodo / Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo provides nutrient data for plain roti prata (per 100 g). Tissue prata is a variant made from the same dough but stretched extremely thin and fried until crispy, then drizzled with honey. The base dough composition is comparable to plain prata; however, the tissue form is fried with more ghee and is crispier, resulting in slightly higher fat and energy per 100 g. Honey contributes additional sugar and carbohydrate. Values for the tissue-with-honey variant are adapted from HPB plain prata data with upward adjustment for fat (additional ghee in frying) and sugar/carbohydrate (honey drizzle), cross-referenced with ICMR-NIN data for Indian flatbreads. The adaptation is documented and conservative.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for flatbread / roti
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for tissue prata with honey in the University of Sydney GI Database. The GI of 57 is estimated from the closest analogous food: plain white-flour flatbread / roti (GI approximately 52–62, mean ~57) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2021 (Am J Clin Nutr) and the University of Sydney GI Database. The honey drizzle adds simple sugars (fructose/glucose blend, GI ~55–58) which is consistent with the estimated composite GI of 57. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (57 × 52.8) / 100 ≈ 30.1, where carbohydrate per 120 g serving = 44.0 × 1.2 = 52.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g; ~372 mg per serving) and saturated fat from ghee
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit tissue prata with honey to occasional consumption, as one serving contributes approximately 16% of the 2300 mg daily sodium limit and the ghee content adds saturated fat that may compound cardiovascular risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 57) combined with high glycemic load per serving (~30) and added honey (simple sugars)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume tissue prata with honey sparingly, as the high glycemic load per serving (~30) and the added honey significantly elevate postprandial blood glucose; portion control and pairing with a protein or fibre source are strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from ghee (~6.8 g per 100 g) and refined white flour base
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit tissue prata with honey due to its saturated fat content from ghee, which may raise LDL cholesterol; choosing smaller portions and reducing frequency of consumption is recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tissue prata with honey is a popular Singaporean-Indian hawker dish consisting of an extremely thin, paper-like crispy flatbread made from a laminated dough of plain wheat flour, water, ghee (clarified butter), egg, and salt. The dough is stretched and spun by skilled cooks until it is almost translucent, then pan-fried in ghee on a flat griddle until it puffs up and crisps into a tall, cone-like or crumpled structure. Once cooked, it is drizzled generously with honey, which adds sweetness and a light floral flavour. The texture is shatteringly crispy on the outside with delicate, paper-thin layers inside. Tissue prata is a showpiece variant of the classic roti prata and is commonly served as a dessert or sweet snack at Indian-Muslim restaurants and mamak stalls across Singapore. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense (~320 kcal per 100 g) with moderate protein (~7 g), significant fat from ghee (~13.5 g, of which ~6.8 g is saturated), and a high carbohydrate load (~44 g per 100 g) elevated further by the honey. The glycemic load per serving is high (~30), making it a food of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. It contains gluten and egg allergens and is not suitable for vegans due to ghee and honey.
common_user_queries: How many calories in tissue prata with honey?
What is the difference between tissue prata and plain prata?

confidence_reason: HPB Foodo provides nutrient data for plain roti prata which is the closest approved primary source for Singapore. Tissue prata with honey is a variant not separately listed in HPB Foodo or ICMR-NIN; values are adapted from plain prata data with documented adjustments for additional ghee and honey. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for white-flour flatbreads. Confidence is medium because no exact match exists in any approved primary source for this specific variant.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo plain roti prata data, adjusted upward for fat (additional ghee used in tissue prata frying technique) and sugar/carbohydrate (honey drizzle contribution), cross-referenced with ICMR-NIN Indian flatbread data. 2. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate based on white-flour flatbread GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 (Am J Clin Nutr); no direct GI measurement for tissue prata with honey was found. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not separately reported in the HPB Foodo plain prata entry used as the source basis. 4. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entry. 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entry. 6. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entry. 7. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entry. 8. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entry. 9. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entry. 10. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 as wheat-flour flatbread with ghee and honey contains no meaningful vitamin C; confirmed zero per HPB and ICMR-NIN flatbread data. 11. Serving size of 120 g is based on typical tissue prata portion weight at Singapore hawker stalls as referenced in HPB portion size guidance. 12. The cuisine_tags in the input included 'Noodles' and 'Drinks & Beverages' which are clearly erroneous for this food item and have been disregarded.
